terms.definition: The degree to which interacting components of a social system maintain mutually reinforcing expectations that minimize conflict and maximize predictability.
Parallel or partially overlapping institutional pathways that can perform the same stabilizing function; inverse proxy for fragility.
A rapid, qualitative shift in system-level ordering following the crossing of a control parameter.
The re-allocation of decision-making power from multiple distributed nodes to a single, hierarchically higher node.
terms.first_use_section: Two Decisions That Centralized Moral Authority

terms.definition: The socially recognized right to define and enforce behavioral norms.
terms.first_use_section: Two Decisions That Centralized Moral Authority

terms.definition: Technologically or institutionally separating two variables that had previously been causally linked; here, sexual activity and reproduction.
The practice of using borrowed capital to amplify present consumption or investment.
The increasing dominance of financial institutions, practices, and motives in the economy and everyday life.
The technological channel that carries symbolic content; distinguished from the content itself.
terms.first_use_section: Television: One Signal, Ninety Percent of Households
